steps.title: Identify the missing main verb
steps.content: The subject is such symbiotic relationships (modified by the 'wherein…' clause). The sentence needs a finite main verb to follow the subject and predicate the action of forming networks.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: finite vs. non-finite verb. The subject is "such symbiotic relationships" (plural) and needs a finite main verb to make a complete sentence. Rule: a main clause requires a finite verb; participles, infinitives, and gerunds cannot serve as the main verb.
  • Choice A — having formed: Perfect participle (non-finite) — leaves the sentence without a main verb; creates a dangling absolute phrase. ✗
  • Choice B — form: Finite plural present verb. Correctly serves as the main verb and agrees with the plural subject "relationships." ✓
  • Choice C — to form: Infinitive (non-finite) — cannot serve as a main verb; creates a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — forming: Present participle (non-finite) — same problem; leaves the sentence without a finite main verb. ✗
B is correct.
